old_claim.note: Fired Bricks & Early Kilns was modeled as an enabling prerequisite for lime plaster.

new_claim_absent: No direct edge remains from lime_plaster to fired_bricks_early_kilns. Source-backed lime plaster chronology predates the current fired-bricks node and depends more generally on controlled fire and pyrotechnic processing, not brick manufacture.

source_shape.source_locator: Article title and introduction identify lime plaster technology 12,000 years ago and discuss early burnt-lime evidence in the southern Levant.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports early lime plaster before the graph's fired_bricks_early_kilns chronology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports lime plaster as an early pyrotechnology but does not require fired bricks or brick-kiln technology as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: The graph made lime plaster chronologically dependent on a later fired-brick/kiln node.
ontology_after: Lime plaster remains downstream of fire control while no longer inheriting a false brick-manufacture dependency.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: fired_bricks_early_kilns is absent as a direct prerequisite for lime_plaster, and lime_plaster remains source-checked to 12,000 BCE chronology.
would_reject_if: A source showed the scoped 12,000 BCE lime plaster required fired brick manufacture or the specific fired_bricks_early_kilns node.
The lime_plaster node were narrowed to later architectural lime plasters that explicitly required kiln infrastructure.
The fired_bricks_early_kilns node were rescoped and redated to cover earlier generic lime-burning pyrotechnology.
short_reason: Early lime plaster predates the fired-bricks node and should not depend on brick manufacture.
